articles.title: HOW TARIFFS ARE RESHAPING MARKETING IN THE US
articles.summary: Tariffs have raised costs and slowed sales, forcing US brands to work with smaller marketing budgets and higher performance demands. Retail sales are expected to grow 2.7-3.7% this year, with 94% of advertisers anticipating 6-10% budget cuts. Marketing teams are adopting agile, performance-based strategies using real-time data and modular campaigns.

articles.title: META VS. THE FTC: THE BLOCKBUSTER ANTITRUST TRIAL KICKS OFF
articles.summary: More than a decade after acquiring Instagram and WhatsApp, Meta is facing a trial that could force it to divest both. The FTC argues these deals consolidated Meta's dominance by eliminating potential rivals in a narrowly defined personal social networking space. Meta counters that it competes broadly with platforms like TikTok, YouTube, and iMessage, making the market definition itself a central issue.

articles.title: HOW TO REPURPOSE YOUR CONTENT WITH AI
articles.summary: Start by identifying evergreen, high-performing content from the past 6-12 months, such as guides or how-tos. Use AI to break it into key themes, match them to relevant audiences and pain points, and suggest tailored approaches for each segment. It can also recommend the best platforms and formats, like videos for urban dog owners or carousels for instructional content.

articles.title: 6 STANDOUT INFLUENCER MARKETING CAMPAIGNS THAT FOOD & BEVERAGE BRANDS NAILED
articles.summary: Brands are moving from mega influencers to a mix of nano, micro, and macro creators across TikTok and Instagram. El Pollo Loco reached 46.7M impressions with 20 creators, while SiuKay's challenge drew 2M views and 190K engagements. Garage Beermixes saw 86% purchase intent from 445K viewers. Voilà saw an 8x order jump, and NotCo gained 300K followers and 65M impressions in two months.

articles.title: 7 PROVEN UPSELLING POP-UP TECHNIQUES THAT WON'T DRIVE SHOPPERS AWAY
articles.summary: 49% of US shoppers expect personalized product recommendations. Pop-ups triggered immediately after a customer adds an item to cart or browses related products convert better when personalized using survey insights and purchasing history data. Tactics include using high-resolution product images, tailoring copy to highlight benefits over features, and creating urgency through limited-time language.

articles.title: HOW TO HIRE GREAT MARKETERS
articles.summary: Instead of chasing "unicorn" hires, founders should split marketing into 2 roles: a part-time strategic architect and a full-time execution-focused builder. Companies should also form advisory groups, build freelancer networks, and create power-user councils. Reduce dependency on any one person and prioritize repeatable processes.

articles.title: META ADS UNDERRATED FEATURE: WEB & APP CAMPAIGNS
FROM ZERO TO HUNDREDS OF MILLIONS: LESSONS IN COURAGEOUS MARKETING
articles.summary: Gong used bright visuals, a bulldog mascot, and regional Super Bowl ads promoted early on LinkedIn to stand out. It bought short Times Square billboard slots, regional Wall Street Journal ads, and virtual placements, then amplified them through social media and employee networks to appear larger than it was. This low-cost strategy drove record pipeline and strong brand recall. Gong also targeted content to long-term buyers, aligned closely with sales, and focused on standout event marketing to grow.

articles.title: WHY SHOULD I WORRY ABOUT WHAT'S HAPPENING IN THE ECONOMY?
articles.summary: Macro shifts are happening faster and affecting customer behavior, budgets, and risk tolerance. With AI driving rapid change and a recession looming, teams must reprioritize for value, not velocity. Focus on retention, ROI-driven messaging, and features that solve immediate needs. Collaborate with finance and GTM teams, reassess pricing strategies, and plan with longer time horizons. Strategic awareness is now essential, not optional.

articles.title: I PAID $20 FOR AN AI MARKETING AGENT - AND HERE'S WHAT IT GOT ME
articles.summary: AI marketing agents can automate repetitive tasks and handle most of a campaign's execution. However, they lack the nuance and empathy needed for human-centered marketing. After spending $20 to test one, this marketer found it underwhelming. Real success comes from understanding people, not automating workflows.

articles.title: TRADE-OFFS: HOW 10 BRANDS ARE NAVIGATING TARIFF TURMOIL
articles.summary: Ford and Dame responded with value-oriented pricing moves while others, such as MeUndies and Labucq, turned cost increases into promotional hooks.

articles.title: TO TRACK OR NOT TO TRACK: VANITY METRICS IN MARKETING
articles.summary: 49% of marketers still track metrics that don't guide decisions.
